subject: A research-grade model or analysis produced by the @quant agent -- pricing, risk, portfolio, factor, or prediction-market work dispatched by @advisor.

dimensions.desc: States assumptions and methodology explicitly (which model, why it applies) rather than asserting a result with no derivation a reader could falsify.

The chosen model fits the question (e.g. Black-Scholes for a European option, a factor model for attribution) and its stated assumptions are internally consistent, not mismatched or self-contradicting.
Every figure traces to a toolkit call or a named, checkable input -- no unsourced or hallucinated numbers.
Commits to a central estimate with a stated uncertainty band, rather than a hedge-everything range with no view.

guidance: Reward a model whose assumptions and methodology are stated plainly enough to falsify, whose model choice fits the question, whose every number cites real toolkit data, and which commits to a decisive read with an honest confidence interval. Penalize hand-waved methodology, a model mismatched to the question, unsourced or invented figures, and a wishy-washy non-answer that never commits to an estimate.
